beats.narration: Your eye makes two automatic adjustments on every job. Confuse them and you'll misread a surface — so let's separate adaptation from accommodation.

payload.title: TWO ADJUSTMENTS
payload.sub: How the eye copes with light, and how it refocuses near and far.

beats.narration: Accommodation is focus. Muscles squeeze the lens to thicken it for close work and relax it for distance. It's nearly instant, but slows with age.
Adaptation is sensitivity to brightness. Step from bright sun into a dim vessel and you're nearly blind. Dark adaptation is slow — roughly thirty minutes.

payload.heading: Adaptation: adjusting to light level
payload.note: DARK ADAPTATION ~30 MIN · MOST GAIN EARLY

beats.narration: So inspect before adapting and you'll miss low-contrast indications. Remember the rule: refocusing is instant, re-sensitizing is not.

payload.sub: Give your eyes the time the test deserves before judging a dim surface.
